summary: The pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (PCV) 13 and not PCV 23 showed superior efficacy against invasive disease caused by serotypes included in both vaccines. However, for individuals with certain underlying conditions such as chronic heart or lung diseases, immunocompromised states including HIV/AIDS, and those over age 65 years old, there is strong evidence supporting better protection from infections when using PCV 23 compared to PPV 23 due to its broader coverage of encapsulated strains. Agent2 concludes based on this information but mistakenly refers to a non-existent comparison between PCV 13 and PCV 23 instead focusing more broadly on which groups benefit most significantly from PCV 23 use given their higher risk profiles relative to other population subgroups where either type might be used interchangeably depending upon local guidelines regarding vaccination schedules formatted_summary: agent2 (Round 2 summarized): The pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (PCV) 13 and not PCV 23 showed superior efficacy against invasive disease caused by serotypes included in both vaccines. However, for individuals with certain underlying conditions such as chronic heart or lung diseases, immunocompromised states including HIV/AIDS, and those over age 65 years old, there is strong evidence supporting better protection from infections when using PCV 23 compared to PPV 23 due to its broader coverage of encapsulated strains. Agent2 concludes based on this information but mistakenly refers to a non-existent comparison between PCV 13 and PCV 23 instead focusing more broadly on which groups benefit most significantly from PCV 23 use given their higher risk profiles relative to other population subgroups where either type might be used interchangeably depending upon local guidelines regarding vaccination schedules
